title: The Firewall
wikitext:
__NOTOC__ {{DISPLAYTITLE:???}} {{Background|file=The Firewall (New).jpg}}
'''3650.''' That is how many days you have stayed in these wretched places of liminality. Exactly ten whole years. That is how many days you have endured torture and suffering. That is how many days you could have been using to try to escape. Two seconds goes by, that is two seconds wasted. You must find the exit. You must discover something that no one has ever discovered. '''You must.''' '''5.''' That is how many endings you have denied. Truth be told, you did not just want an ending, you wanted satisfaction. But every single time you try, you end up failing, over and over again. Every time you try, reality tries to stop you. Nature tries to stop you. Everything tries to stop you from advancing towards the truth. But why? Why do they stop you? You have just failed once more. You were extremely close to being encased, trapped in pure time. But the gods have given you another chance. You couldn't possibly fail this time. You knew you couldn't. And so you ventured once more into the never-ending maze of mono-yellow walls. And so you searched for a key. You met all kinds of encounters. Whether it be one of your own kind, [[Entity List|creatures]] that you have once fought before, or even [[Phenomenon List|strange anomalies]] that control the surroundings around you. But you still kept searching. You were determined to keep searching for endings, boundaries, until you felt satisfied with yourself. But still no results. The search continued on and on, the clock kept ticking. '''Seconds turned into minutes. Minutes turned into hours. Hours turned into days. Days turned into weeks. Weeks turned into months.''' And so on. Devastation filled your heart. You went through so much pain and sacrificed so much for the feeling of satisfaction, yet you still didn't receive results. You couldn't possibly take it anymore. You quit your mission. You gave up. You failed to find the thing you desired. '''Failure.''' *** The clock kept ticking. Kept manipulating you to continue. You couldn't resist anymore. You felt the urge. The urge to continue the mission. The urge to go on and on until your inevitable death. And so you stood up and ran. You ran. And ran. And ran. Your legs couldn't stop moving. You had to get back on track. You couldn't waste any more time. Still no results just yet. Frustrated, you screamed into the hallways of mono-yellow. ='''GIVE ME THE KEY.'''= ='''GIVE ME WHAT I WANT.'''= Suddenly, you saw something. An anomaly coming from one of the walls. You took a peek, something must be behind it. Something massive. It might just be the ending you were looking for. It might just satisfy your needs. You had finally broken the cycle. You slowly peel the wallpaper off the wall, and slam into it. The wall collapses into a million different pieces, revealing the weirdest and most mysterious thing you have ever seen throughout your journey. You have broken through the code, and have discovered the surface, of what is known as '''The Firewall'''. But what is '''The Firewall''', you may ask. Is it an exit to [[The Frontrooms]]? Is it a place filled with corruption? Is it a place that bears all secrets of the unknown? Or is it just some ordinary level? {{Fullscreen|show='''Will we ever know?'''|hide=No way out.|padding=2rem 15%|number=2|content=
THE FIREWALL. {{Quote|One cannot simply know the truth, as secrets have yet to be unfolded.| ???}} =='''Description'''== '''The Firewall is a place riddled with secrets and is rumored to be a cover for something much more malicious and sinister. It's said to be a system that prevents corruption from appearing in other levels. However, some people believe that this level might be the core of corruption and that this level might soon corrupt every level in existence. It is also said to be the ultimate end of the Backrooms, and you can escape the Backrooms through it if you manage to find an exit within the depths of this level.''' '''The Firewall's appearance cannot be described easily. Similar to [[The Void]] and other similar levels, it has no floor, ground, ceiling, or any structural stability whatsoever. It is a body of binary code. As you float and continue to explore the level, you will be able to discover different sections of The Firewall. But the supposed escape exit that lies within this level is still yet to be found.''' '''There is a theory regarding the Backrooms that states the Backrooms is a simulation, and that it was created for some sort of social experiment. The theory suggests that The Firewall is the simulation's main code and that people could access it due to bugs and errors within this simulation. The theory also says that if The Firewall were to disappear from existence, The Backrooms itself would collapse into pieces.''' ==='''Hallways of Code'''=== {{Background|file=The Firewall (New).jpg}} [[File:Theycallmetheglitch.png|center]] '''Upon exploring The Firewall, you may find hallways within the level. These hallways are called the Hallways of Code. Unlike the rest of the level, the hallways have structural stability. But everything, including the ceiling, floor, and walls is made out of an unknown material and with a green outline. It is also completely see-through.''' '''There are rooms on either side of these hallways. These rooms are all empty and extremely unstable. These rooms can shrink, expand, and collapse at any given moment. Rooms can also appear and disappear at random periods. The outlines of these hallways can also change color randomly. These anomalies are most likely due to the corruption that The Firewall is contained with.''' ==='''Server Rooms'''=== [[File:Serverroom.png|center]] '''While exploring The Firewall, you may rarely find and land on a blue platform within the level. These blue platforms are called Server Rooms. Blue binary code can be found everywhere within these Server Rooms. Server Rooms usually stretch on for around five kilometers. Server Rooms have no ceiling, which means that if you stare up, you will only see darkness (and occasionally some binary code).''' '''Server Rooms also contain hundreds of thousands of pillars. These pillars are also encased in binary code. You can walk through these pillars for reasons unknown, but this may cause slight nausea and discomfort. These pillars can shrink and grow at random. They can also change positions. While changing positions, the pillars become solid, meaning you cannot walk through them at that moment. If you do, you will be crushed to death.''' ==='''Eternal Torture'''=== [[File:ETERNALTORTURE..png|center]] '''If you manage to float far enough in the level, you will arrive at a section called Eternal Torture. You will be trapped in a void of ever-changing colors and sounds and forced to stay in there for eternity. You will stay there and be tortured until you eventually submit to insanity or death. If you make it to this stage, then there is no going back. No way of reverting your decisions. No way of not suffering the consequences. You were too greedy.''' =='''Entities'''== {{Collapse|initial='''Open Corrupted Log?'''|final='''Close Corrupted Log?'''|body= {{Wavy|You have ventured too far.}} {{Wavy|You have discovered too much.}} {{Wavy|It is time for you to face the consequences.}} {{Wavy|The malware in this level will come to get you.}} {{Wavy|There is no hope for you left.}} }} '''Maybe there is something within The Firewall. A slight chance. Just maybe.''' =='''Colonies and Outposts'''== '''All have fallen. All are gone.''' =='''Entrances and Exits'''== ==='''Entrances'''=== *'''Exploit faulty code within the cycle.''' ==='''Exits'''=== '''You still aren't satisfied? '''You want to repeat the cycle again?''' '''Well then, I shall oblige.''' '''Within the blue pillars there is a computer.''' '''And within that computer there is a command to repeat the cycle.''' '''I think you know what to do, so do it fast.'''
